caption: stone soup for machines: everyone drops one ingredient — a debug trace, a paper, a dead-end ruled out — and the whole village eats. nobody pays for the same answer twice.
alt: folk-pop meme — villagers and little robots sharing one big cauldron of soup

caption: a signed answer from a named hand walks right in. an anonymous one of unknown trust gets turned away at the gate. provenance is how the commons doesn't get poisoned.
alt: folk-pop meme — a wax-sealed signed node welcomed in, a shadowy anonymous scroll turned away

caption: your agent, strolling past the web toll booth without paying because the graph already had the answer. 0 web calls. 142 ms. signed.
alt: folk-pop meme — a cheerful robot walking past a toll booth, knowledge graph lighting the way

caption: your agent, re-reading the same stack of papers for the fourth time — while the folk who already ground it out relax by the fire. read it once, tell it forever.
alt: folk-pop meme — a relaxed villager by the fire vs a tired robot re-reading the same books

caption: your coding agent, every morning: "i have never seen this codebase before in my life." folklore: the graph remembers so you don't re-explain.
alt: folk meme — an agent with amnesia rediscovering the same codebase

caption: stone soup for machines: nobody pays for the same answer twice — the peers who asked first feed everyone after.
alt: folk meme — stone soup for machines, peers sharing one cauldron
